MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Feb-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TIBCO SOFTWARE SINGAPORE PTE LTD. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is DENIED. At a minimum, the hiring process for plaintiff's position with the moving defendant - a focus of many of his claims - was conducted in California. The service of process was valid. Order signed. Judge: Richard Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Sara Lerschen, CSR No. 6213, email: SLerschen@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Based On Improper Venue; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-15-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court confirmed its tentative ruling. Defendants TIBCO Software, Inc., Rajnish Verma, and William Hughes' motion to dismiss is denied. In their initial memorandum the moving defendants contend that there are three grounds why this case should be dismissed: 1) the doctrine of forum nonconveniens; 2) TIBCO Singapore is a necessary and indispensable party; and 3) FEHA's venue provision establishes that San Francisco is not a proper venue for this case. The moving defendants have not shown that the case should be dismissed under any of these grounds. Moving defendants have not met their burden of overcoming the strong presumption in favor of plaintiff Jonathan Oomrigar's choice of a California forum for his claims. (National Football League v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co., 216 Cal.App.4th 902, 932.) Assuming without deciding that Singapore is a suitable forum for Mr. Oomirigar's claims against all defendants, the public and private factors weigh heavily in retaining this case in California. Mr. Oomrigar and all moving defendants are California residents and most, if not all, of Mr. Oomrigar's claims are based in substantial part on conduct that took place in California. Moving defendants' necessary and indispensable party argument has been mooted by the purported service of TIBCO Singapore. While defendants apparently believe that the service on TIBCO Singapore was ineffective, that argument was raised for the first time in moving defendants' reply papers and thus may not be considered on this motion. If and when TIBCO Singapore is successful on a motion to quash service on it, moving defendants may bring a motion re-asserting their necessary and indispensable party arguments. However, for guidance of the parties, the court is not persuaded based on the record on this motion that, even if TIBCO Singapore cannot be made a party to this lawsuit, equity and good conscience per CCP 387(b) precludes this lawsuit from proceeding in the absence of TIBCO Singapore. Assuming without deciding that FEHA's venue provision establishes that that San Francisco is not a proper venue for this case, moving defendants' remedy is not dismissal, but transfer of the case to a county where there is proper venue under FEHA. Order signed.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Denise Doucette, CSR #5963. (302/HK)
